dial.summary: Global Liquidity holds near $101.9T and ISM stays at a four-year high of 54.0% (no new release until July 1), while the Fed balance sheet sits flat near $6.73T — a supportive liquidity backdrop even as real yields (DFII10 2.17%) stay a headwind above 2% on a 4-week basis and the 2s10s curve holds a flattening bias at +0.40% (10Y 4.49% / 2Y 4.09%, last Friday's close); ICE DXY eased to ~99.56 today — still up ~1.3% over the past month but neutral between 97.5 and 100 — BTC funding remains a barely-positive ~0.0031% (supportive, last read June 10), and stablecoin dominance held near 13.74% while BTC dominance firmed slightly to 56.6% and TOTAL held around $2.27T, keeping the setup consistent with BTC Accumulation as markets navigate today's BOJ decision (Governor Ueda hospitalized, Deputies Himino/Uchida presiding), tomorrow's FOMC Day 1, and this week's Fed (June 17) and BOE (June 18) decisions.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: MacroMicro's Big-Four (Fed+ECB+BOJ+PBOC, dollar-denominated) global M2 estimate holds at roughly $101.9T as of the June 12, 2026 print, with the next weekly update pending, extending the steady multi-month climb from ~$100.86T in March and ~$98.6T in early February; 4-week trend remains up; status supportive.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: No new release since June 1; May 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI remains the latest at 54.0% — the highest since May 2022 and a fifth straight month of expansion (New Orders 56.8%, Production 54.3%); 4-week trend up (April 52.7% to May 54.0%); status supportive (above 50, rising, well below the 62 overheating line); the June-data report is due July 1, 2026.

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY eased to ~99.56 on June 15, 2026, down about 0.19% from Friday's close as markets position for the BOJ (June 15-16) and FOMC (June 16-17) decisions, though it still sits near its recent two-month high after the broad dollar index gained roughly 1.3-1.4% over the past month, putting the 4-week trend up; status stays neutral with ICE DXY between the 97.5 and 100 thresholds ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC.

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: DFII10 (10-year TIPS real yield) remains at 2.17% as of the June 12, 2026 close, the latest available print with markets closed over the weekend, up roughly 24bps from ~1.93% in early May; 4-week trend remains up; status stays headwind (above 2% and still rising on a 4-week basis), with the June 16-17 FOMC dot-plot the key catalyst for whether real yields can retreat toward the 2% threshold.

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: The 10-year Treasury closed at 4.49% and the 2-year at 4.09% on June 12, 2026 (latest available, no new prints over the weekend), keeping the spread at +0.40%, down from roughly +0.43-0.48% across May; the 4-week comparison shows continued flattening, so trend is down; status remains neutral (positive but flattening, far from the < -50bps deep-inversion headwind) ahead of the June 16-17 FOMC dot-plot.

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: The June 11, 2026 H.4.1 release (data as of June 10) still shows total assets of roughly $6.725T, essentially unchanged from ~$6.728T about four weeks ago; the next weekly release is not due until June 18, so the figure carries over; QT remains paused and the balance sheet continues to hold in a narrow range; status supportive (stable, no aggressive QT alongside steady rates).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: The network-wide 8-hour average BTC perpetual funding rate was last reliably read at roughly 0.0031% as of June 10, 2026 (CoinGlass/ChainCatcher), down from ~0.0094%/8h a month ago, and a fresher mid-June figure could not be sourced today (CoinGlass unreachable); 4-week trend down but funding remains modestly positive; status supportive — still well below the 0.04% persistently-elevated headwind threshold, consistent with a de-leveraged market.

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap holds near $311B at 13.74% of the ~$2.27T total crypto market (CoinGecko), essentially flat versus yesterday's 13.72% but still up sharply from ~12.3% a month ago, so the 4-week trend remains up; status neutral — the headwind condition needs BOTH rising dominance AND a rising TOTAL market cap, but TOTAL is roughly flat-to-down (-0.59% today) near $2.27T rather than breaking higher, so that criterion is not met; BTC dominance held firm near 56.6%.
